title: Labordia cyrtandrae
intro: Labordia cyrtandrae, also known as Kulu'ku'u, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands, belonging to the family Loganiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Labordia cyrtandrae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, Labordia cyrtandrae is often prepared as a tea or infused oil to treat various ailments, and is also used in topical applications to promote wound healing and skin health.
